Statement_Summary: APPSolve scope covers design / build / test / cutover / hypercare of OIC integrations only — not endpoint systems
Risk_If_Invalid: Scope creep into endpoint system support or administration

Statement_Summary: One integration = one directional data flow between one source and one target system
Risk_If_Invalid: Client counts an A-to-B-to-A round-trip as one integration; APPSolve counts it as two

Statement_Summary: Integration Inventory is the authoritative scope document — agreed in Scope and Design phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Scope disputes at delivery if Integration Inventory not signed off

Statement_Summary: Client holds valid OIC Enterprise or Standard licence and provides provisioned environment
Risk_If_Invalid: Development cannot commence without OIC environment

Statement_Summary: OIC Enterprise (B91110) includes a non-production OIC environment as standard
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to procure or provision a separate non-production environment

Statement_Summary: Integrations not in the signed Integration Inventory are a Change Request
Risk_If_Invalid: Client adds integrations during build without formal Change Request

Statement_Summary: Where OIC accelerators apply their effort reduction is reflected in the estimate
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects full standard effort when an accelerator was used and disputes estimate basis

Statement_Summary: Oracle-to-Oracle integrations use native Oracle REST APIs / OIC / OTBI — mechanism confirmed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: Bespoke connectors built where native Oracle connectivity would have sufficed

Statement_Summary: Default architecture is point-to-point — one source to one target via OIC
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes hub-and-spoke or ESB architecture is included

Statement_Summary: Orchestration integrations (3+ systems) are separately scoped — not in standard point-to-point scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Complex multi-system orchestration baked into estimate as point-to-point effort

Statement_Summary: Standard Oracle adapters used — custom adapter development excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Non-Oracle systems with no published API require bespoke adapter — not in standard scope

Statement_Summary: APPSolve produces Technical Design Document (TDD) per integration — signed off before build
Risk_If_Invalid: Build commences without agreed design causing rework

Statement_Summary: SFTP file-based integration is default fallback where REST/SOAP unavailable
Risk_If_Invalid: No integration approach available for systems without API or SFTP — scope gap discovered late
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm endpoint supports SFTP if REST/SOAP not available

Statement_Summary: REST preferred over SOAP where both available — protocol documented in Integration Inventory
Risk_If_Invalid: Legacy SOAP selected by default when REST is available and faster to implement

Statement_Summary: Applicable OIC accelerators reduce design effort — accelerators and effort reduction confirmed in estimate
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes estimate is too high when accelerator was available but effort reduction not documented

Statement_Summary: Error handling included in every integration design — standard error pattern applied
Risk_If_Invalid: Integrations fail silently in production; errors not surfaced or retried

Statement_Summary: Standard retry logic included (3 retries with exponential backoff) — custom retry rules assessed per complexity
Risk_If_Invalid: Integration retries indefinitely on transient failures; or does not retry at all

Statement_Summary: All endpoint systems are available and accessible during SIT and UAT
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT deferred due to endpoint unavailability — project delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Ensure endpoints available in agreed testing windows

Statement_Summary: Client provides all endpoint technical connection details before design phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Design cannot be completed without endpoint specifications
Customer_Action_Required: Provide endpoint specs (URL / credentials / API docs) before design

Statement_Summary: Third-party vendor provides API docs / sandbox / technical support — client manages vendor relationship
Risk_If_Invalid: Vendor not responsive; integration cannot be tested; APPSolve held responsible for delay
Customer_Action_Required: Engage vendor and obtain API docs and sandbox access

Statement_Summary: Where no vendor sandbox exists APPSolve uses mock data during dev — live endpoint available for SIT
Risk_If_Invalid: Integration passes mock-based tests but fails against real endpoint in SIT
Customer_Action_Required: Ensure live endpoint available for SIT

Statement_Summary: Third-party endpoint unavailability during testing is client's risk — APPSolve not responsible for vendor-caused delays
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve held responsible for project timeline when vendor is the blocker
Customer_Action_Required: Manage vendor availability for testing windows

Statement_Summary: Oracle Fusion HCM/ERP endpoints use Oracle standard REST APIs — custom Oracle endpoints require assessment
Risk_If_Invalid: Custom APEX or bespoke PaaS APIs assumed to follow Oracle standard patterns

Statement_Summary: Client IT team responsible for all endpoint security configuration — firewall / whitelisting / VPN
Risk_If_Invalid: Firewall blocks OIC traffic; connectivity fails; project delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Complete all firewall and network changes before SIT

Statement_Summary: All firewall rule changes completed before SIT commencement
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT delayed by pending firewall approvals — timeline impact
Customer_Action_Required: Complete firewall changes before SIT start date

Statement_Summary: Client coordinates IP whitelisting of OIC IP ranges at third-party endpoints
Risk_If_Invalid: Third-party endpoint blocks OIC — SIT fails for connectivity reasons not build defects
Customer_Action_Required: Coordinate IP whitelisting with vendor before SIT

Statement_Summary: APPSolve responsible for OIC connection configuration only — network-layer security is client IT responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to troubleshoot client network or vendor firewall issues

Statement_Summary: Default authentication is OAuth 2.0 (or Basic Auth where OAuth unavailable) — alternatives documented per endpoint
Risk_If_Invalid: Non-standard auth (JWT / mTLS / custom token) assumed to be same effort as OAuth — estimate understated

Statement_Summary: Client IT provisions service accounts for OIC connectivity before build phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Build cannot begin without service account credentials
Customer_Action_Required: Provision service accounts and provide credentials

Statement_Summary: Client procures all SSL/TLS certificates required for HTTPS connectivity
Risk_If_Invalid: Connectivity fails due to invalid or missing certificate
Customer_Action_Required: Procure and provide all certificates before SIT

Statement_Summary: All certificates provided to APPSolve before SIT commencement
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT deferred due to missing certificates
Customer_Action_Required: Provide certificates before SIT start date

Statement_Summary: Delay in certificate availability delays SIT — timeline impact is client's risk
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve held responsible for timeline delay caused by client's certificate procurement delay

Statement_Summary: APPSolve installs and configures certificates in OIC — procurement and endpoint-side installation is client's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage entire certificate lifecycle
Customer_Action_Required: Install certificates in endpoint systems if required

Statement_Summary: Post-go-live certificate expiry management is client IT team's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Certificate expires post-go-live; integration fails; APPSolve expected to renew without AMS agreement
Customer_Action_Required: Monitor certificate expiry and renew before expiry

Statement_Summary: Business mapping decisions (source field to target field) are the client's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Mapping workshops stall when client team cannot make business decisions
Customer_Action_Required: Attend mapping workshops and make mapping decisions

Statement_Summary: APPSolve facilitates mapping workshops and produces the Mapping Specification
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to produce the mapping without client input
Customer_Action_Required: Attend and contribute to mapping workshops

Statement_Summary: Signed Mapping Specification is mandatory prerequisite before build commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Build starts without signed mapping — rework required when client changes mapping during build
Customer_Action_Required: Review and sign off Mapping Specification

Statement_Summary: APPSolve implements transformation logic (lookups / calculations / conditional logic) per approved Mapping Specification
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve makes independent business transformation decisions causing incorrect data

Statement_Summary: Client provides complete code-set translation tables before mapping workshop
Risk_If_Invalid: Mapping workshop incomplete; cannot build translation lookups without client codes
Customer_Action_Required: Provide all code-set mappings before mapping workshop

Statement_Summary: Changes to approved Mapping Specification after build commencement are a Change Request
Risk_If_Invalid: Client changes field mappings mid-build and expects rework at no additional cost

Statement_Summary: Two test cycles standard — SIT (APPSolve executes) and UAT (client executes with APPSolve support)
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to execute UAT on client's behalf
Customer_Action_Required: Appoint UAT testers and execute UAT

Statement_Summary: Client provides all test data for SIT and UAT before testing commences
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT cannot proceed without test data — project delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Provide complete test data before testing starts

Statement_Summary: APPSolve executes SIT; client executes UAT with APPSolve support
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to both execute and manage UAT

Statement_Summary: Defect classification: (a) build defects — APPSolve fixes; (b) data defects — client; (c) mapping defects — mapping change; (d) vendor defects — vendor
Risk_If_Invalid: All defects assigned to APPSolve regardless of root cause

Statement_Summary: Client provides written sign-off at each testing milestone before next phase begins
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve proceeds to production without client sign-off — disputed go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Provide written sign-off at SIT completion and UAT completion

Statement_Summary: Client manages third-party vendor participation in SIT — vendor unavailability is client's risk
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT delayed by vendor non-attendance — APPSolve held responsible

Statement_Summary: OIC integrations designed for normal business transaction volumes as specified in Integration Inventory
Risk_If_Invalid: Actual production volumes exceed estimated volumes — performance issues post-go-live

Statement_Summary: Load testing and stress testing excluded from standard scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects load testing to be included in standard OIC engagement

Statement_Summary: Performance tuning beyond standard OIC configuration excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to optimise OIC infrastructure performance — Oracle manages OIC platform
Customer_Action_Required: Raise with Oracle Support if platform-level performance issues

Statement_Summary: OIC performance subject to Oracle OIC published platform limits — not configurable by APPSolve
Risk_If_Invalid: Integration throughput hits Oracle platform limit — not an APPSolve design defect
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm OIC licence tier provides sufficient message capacity

Statement_Summary: Client provides accurate transaction volume estimates before design phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Underestimated volumes lead to integration design inadequate for actual production load
Customer_Action_Required: Provide transaction volume estimates (messages per hour / file sizes / peak periods)

Statement_Summary: One production cutover event included per integration
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects multiple production rehearsals in base scope

Statement_Summary: Rollback plan included for each integration — documented in cutover runbook
Risk_If_Invalid: No rollback mechanism designed; integration cannot be reversed if production go-live fails

Statement_Summary: Multiple production cutover rehearsals excluded from standard scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects 2-3 dress rehearsals before go-live without additional charge
Customer_Action_Required: Separately contract dress rehearsals if required

Statement_Summary: Production cutover requires client's written go-live authorisation — APPSolve does not deploy without authorisation
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve deploys to production without authority — client disputes production deployment timing
Customer_Action_Required: Provide written go-live authorisation before production deployment

Statement_Summary: End-to-end data reconciliation at cutover is client's responsibility — APPSolve validates OIC processing
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to reconcile business data across source and target
Customer_Action_Required: Perform business-level data reconciliation at cutover

Statement_Summary: Standard OIC monitoring configured — Activity Stream / Error Management console / Alerts
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects a custom monitoring portal or integration health dashboard

Statement_Summary: OIC error notification emails configured — recipients confirmed in Scope and Design
Risk_If_Invalid: No one receives error alerts — integration failures go undetected in production
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm error notification recipients in Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Custom monitoring dashboards excluded from standard scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects a bespoke integration monitoring portal

Statement_Summary: Third-party monitoring platform integration (Splunk / Dynatrace / New Relic) excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects OIC errors to flow into their enterprise monitoring platform without separate scoping

Statement_Summary: Post-go-live monitoring ownership is client IT operations team — APPSolve not actively monitoring post-hypercare
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to monitor and respond to OIC errors indefinitely post-project

Statement_Summary: OIC hypercare aligns to HCM Base standard — 4 weeks / business hours / defect resolution only
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects 24x7 integration support in standard hypercare

Statement_Summary: OIC hypercare runs concurrently with primary module hypercare when delivered in same project
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects separate OIC hypercare period after primary module hypercare ends

Statement_Summary: Integration enhancements during hypercare excluded (new fields / new integrations / changed frequency)
Risk_If_Invalid: Client uses hypercare as an integration change request resource

Statement_Summary: Third-party vendor API changes during/after hypercare are outside APPSolve hypercare obligation
Risk_If_Invalid: Vendor upgrades API version; integration breaks; APPSolve expected to fix under hypercare
Customer_Action_Required: Separately contract API change management if required

Statement_Summary: Client appoints business owner per integration — accountable for requirements / mapping / UAT sign-off
Risk_If_Invalid: No business owner; mapping decisions cannot be made; project stalls
Customer_Action_Required: Appoint business owner per integration before Scope and Design

Statement_Summary: Client appoints UAT testers per integration and ensures availability during agreed UAT window
Risk_If_Invalid: No UAT testers available — UAT deferred; project delayed
Customer_Action_Required: Appoint UAT testers and confirm availability

Statement_Summary: Client ensures all endpoint systems technically ready and accessible before SIT
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT fails due to endpoint system not configured or deployed in test environment
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm endpoint system readiness before SIT

Statement_Summary: Client IT completes all firewall / IP whitelist / VPN / ACL changes before SIT
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT fails on connectivity; client IT team's changes are behind schedule
Customer_Action_Required: Complete all network security changes at least 5 business days before SIT

Statement_Summary: Client procures and provides all SSL/TLS certificates before SIT — certificates valid and correctly formatted
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT fails on certificate error; procurement not started
Customer_Action_Required: Procure and provide certificates before SIT

Statement_Summary: Client ensures all integration endpoints available during agreed testing windows — notifies APPSolve of maintenance 5 business days in advance
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT/UAT window lost due to planned maintenance not communicated
Customer_Action_Required: Notify APPSolve of endpoint maintenance at least 5 business days in advance

Statement_Summary: Client manages commercial and technical relationship with all third-party vendors — including API docs / sandbox / SIT coordination
Risk_If_Invalid: Vendor non-responsive; APPSolve expected to manage vendor escalation
Customer_Action_Required: Manage all vendor relationships and ensure vendor participation in SIT

Statement_Summary: Client validates business accuracy of data flowing through integrations during UAT
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to validate business accuracy as well as technical accuracy
Customer_Action_Required: Perform business data validation during UAT

Statement_Summary: Client provides written sign-off at: mapping specification / TDD / SIT completion / UAT completion / go-live authorisation
Risk_If_Invalid: Project proceeds to next phase without formal sign-off; scope disputes at go-live
Customer_Action_Required: Provide written sign-off at each milestone

Statement_Summary: Client IT provisions service accounts before build phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Build blocked waiting for service account credentials
Customer_Action_Required: Provision service accounts and confirm credentials

Statement_Summary: Client obtains and provides complete API documentation from all third-party vendors before design phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Design phase cannot be completed without vendor API specs
Customer_Action_Required: Obtain and provide vendor API documentation before design

Statement_Summary: Client holds valid OIC licence; environment provisioned and accessible before development commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Development cannot start; client expects APPSolve to provision OIC environment
Customer_Action_Required: Ensure OIC is provisioned and accessible before project start

Statement_Summary: OIC Enterprise licence active and OIC environment provisioned before integration development commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Development deferred due to OIC provisioning delay

Statement_Summary: All source and target technical specs finalised before integration design completed
Risk_If_Invalid: Design changes during build — rework required; scope dispute

Statement_Summary: Oracle HCM/ERP modules sufficiently configured before SIT of dependent integrations commences
Risk_If_Invalid: SIT fails because HCM/ERP module has no test data; integration cannot execute

Statement_Summary: Third-party vendor API availability and documentation available before design phase
Risk_If_Invalid: Design cannot proceed for integrations where vendor has not provided specs

Statement_Summary: Mapping Specification and TDD signed off before build commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Build produces incorrect output because mapping was not approved

Statement_Summary: Production credentials available at least 5 business days before cutover
Risk_If_Invalid: Cutover deferred because production credentials not provided

Statement_Summary: APPSolve configures OIC only — does not configure / administer / support endpoint systems on either side
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to troubleshoot issues in SAP / PaySpace / SuccessFactors / other endpoints

Statement_Summary: Integration performance subject to Oracle OIC platform published limits — APPSolve cannot override
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects integration to process volumes beyond Oracle OIC platform limits

Statement_Summary: OIC requires Oracle adapter or REST/SOAP/SFTP/DB API at endpoint — no custom adapter development
Risk_If_Invalid: System with no API and no SFTP cannot be integrated without custom adapter development

Statement_Summary: APPSolve cannot guarantee third-party vendor API uptime / versioning / documentation quality
Risk_If_Invalid: Vendor API changes or goes down; client expects APPSolve to fix at no cost

Statement_Summary: Integration scope bounded by signed Integration Inventory — additions require Change Request
Risk_If_Invalid: Client adds integrations during project without Change Request; estimate exceeded

Statement_Summary: Integration complexity classified as Simple / Standard / Complex — tier determines effort estimate
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes estimate claiming their complex integration should be priced as simple

Statement_Summary: Data cleansing of source system data excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to fix bad data in source system as part of integration project

Statement_Summary: Source system remediation excluded — defects / data issues / API defects in source systems are source system owner's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to fix bugs in SAP / PaySpace / legacy system to enable integration

Statement_Summary: Third-party vendor management excluded — client owns vendor commercial relationship
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to chase vendor for API docs / sandbox / support

Statement_Summary: Infrastructure procurement excluded — servers / networks / cloud infra beyond OIC is client's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to procure SFTP server / API gateway / additional cloud infrastructure

Statement_Summary: Network troubleshooting excluded — client IT / vendor IT responsible for network issues at their layer
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to diagnose and resolve client network or vendor firewall blocking OIC

Statement_Summary: Business process redesign excluded — OIC scope is technical integration not process redesign
Risk_If_Invalid: Integration triggers a process redesign conversation; APPSolve expected to lead it

Statement_Summary: Change management and user adoption for integrated business processes excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage change communications for all systems affected by integrations

Statement_Summary: Training beyond agreed super-user / admin scope excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects broad end-user training on all integrated systems

Statement_Summary: Managed services / post-hypercare ongoing support excluded from standard implementation contract
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to continue monitoring and managing integrations indefinitely

Statement_Summary: 24x7 support excluded from standard implementation and hypercare
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects round-the-clock integration support during and after project

Statement_Summary: EDI and AS2 integrations excluded from standard OIC scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes EDI integrations are included in standard integration scope

Statement_Summary: Custom proprietary protocol integrations excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes any endpoint can be integrated with standard OIC effort

Statement_Summary: ESB / message broker replacement programmes excluded from standard OIC scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes OIC can replace MuleSoft / Tibco / BizTalk as a project activity

Statement_Summary: Load testing and performance benchmarking excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects load testing included in OIC project

Statement_Summary: Custom monitoring dashboards and third-party monitoring platform integration excluded
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects OIC errors to flow into Splunk / Dynatrace / custom portal
